channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump allies tensions likely to outlast Iran war
item.description: President Trump pulled troops from Germany and downplayed Iran attacks on Gulf partners, signaling fraying ties with key allies that could outlast the conflict. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia holds scaled-back Victory Day parade amid Ukraine war
item.description: Russia held its most scaled-back Victory Day parade in years due to threat of attack from Ukraine, where victory has proven elusive four years into the deadliest European conflict since WWII. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇮🇷 US expects Iran response to peace proposal tonight
item.description: President Trump said he expects Iran response to a US peace proposal soon, while US and Iran continue exchanging fire in the Strait of Hormuz. (CNN)

item.title: 🕊️ Russia and Ukraine agree to 3-day ceasefire
item.description: President Trump announced both sides agreed to a three-day ceasefire coinciding with Russia Victory Day celebrations, including a prisoner exchange of 1,000 from each country. (AP News)

item.title: ⚔️ US strikes Iranian ships in Gulf of Oman
item.description: US Central Command released video showing F/A-18 Super Hornets using precision munitions against two Iranian tankers as fighting continues around the Strait of Hormuz. (CBS News)

item.title: 🇧🇾 Belarus Expands Military Infrastructure With Russian Coordination
item.description: Ukrainian border officials report Belarus is developing training grounds and logistics routes that could support Russian operations against Ukraine, though no immediate offensive threat exists. (Kyiv Independent)

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Signs Record €44 Billion EU Defense Loan
item.description: Poland becomes the largest beneficiary of the EU SAFE program, securing preferential loans for defense modernization as Europe accelerates rearmament amid Russian threat and concerns over US commitment. (France 24)

item.title: 🇪🇺 Russia Still Outproducing EU Militarily, Defence Commissioner Warns
item.description: EU Defence Commissioner Andrius Kubilius says Russia produces more ammunition in three months than NATO produces in a year, urging European defense industry to scale up faster. (Euronews)

item.title: 💻🌐🏫 Global Cyberattack Disrupts Thousands of Schools and Universities Worldwide
item.description: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for a major ransomware attack on Canvas, the academic software platform used by approximately 9,000 educational institutions globally. Students across the US, Canada, Australia and Europe faced exam disruptions as ransom demands appeared on screens during final exam season. (BBC)

item.title: 🇭🇺🏛️ Hungary Swears in New Prime Minister After Viktor Orban Era Ends
item.description: Peter Magyar of the Tisza party is being sworn in as Hungary's new prime minister after a landslide victory ended 16 years of Viktor Orban's rule. The new government faces economic challenges and must negotiate the release of €17 billion in frozen EU funds. (BBC)

item.title: 🇿🇦⚖️ South African Opposition Calls for Ramaphosa Resignation After Court Ruling
item.description: South Africa's Constitutional Court ruled that parliament violated the constitution by blocking impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over the Farmgate scandal involving stolen cash at his farm. Opposition leader Julius Malema called for Ramaphosa to resign. (BBC)

item.title: 🇬🇷🚢💥 Mystery Naval Drone With Explosives Found in Greek Waters
item.description: Greek military is examining an unmanned naval drone discovered by fishermen near the island of Lefkada. Reports suggest the vessel may be Ukrainian-made and could be linked to operations against Russia's "shadow fleet" or the broader Ukraine-Russia conflict. (BBC)

item.title: 🇭🇰 Jimmy Lai's Fate May Turn on Trump-Xi Meeting Next Week
item.description: The imprisoned Hong Kong pro-democracy activist's family hopes Trump will seek his release during an upcoming Beijing summit. The 78-year-old media mogul was sentenced to 20 years under China's national security law, and observers say his release could signal whether Beijing is ready to move on. (AP News)

item.title: 🔋🇪🇹 Africa's EV Surge Led by Ethiopia as Fuel Shortages Mount
item.description: Africa imported 44,358 electric vehicles from China in 2025, up from 19,386 the prior year, with Ethiopia leading adoption after banning new fossil fuel vehicle imports. The Iran war's disruption of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz has accelerated the transition away from costly fuel imports. (AP News)

item.title: 🇵🇸 Israel Wounds 9 Palestinians Including Child in Gaza Ceasefire Violation
item.description: Gaza Civil Defense reports that Israeli forces wounded 9 Palestinians, including a child, in the latest violation of the ceasefire agreement. (TRT World)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇱🇧 US to Host Israel-Lebanon Talks Pressing for Hezbollah Disarmament
item.description: Washington will host new Israel-Lebanon negotiations as the Trump team pushes for Hezbollah disarmament, while awaiting Iran's response to a US proposal to end regional conflict. (SCMP)

item.title: 🇯🇵🇳🇿 Japan's Mogami-Class Warship Favored for New Zealand Frigate Competition
item.description: Japan's Mogami-class frigate has emerged as the leading contender for New Zealand's next-generation warship program, competing against Britain's Type 31 design, with Australia potentially influencing the outcome. (SCMP)

item.title: 🇮🇱 S&P Maintains Israel Credit Rating at A Despite Iran War
item.description: S&P Global Ratings affirmed Israel's A/A-1 credit rating with stable outlook, citing resilience despite ongoing Iran conflict and regional tensions. (Times of Israel)

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Appeals for Calm After Vatican Meeting With Rubio
item.description: Pope Leo asked God to inspire world leaders to reduce tensions and hatred in an address marking his first anniversary as head of the Catholic Church, following his meeting with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio amid strained U.S.-Holy See relations. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇮🇹 Rubio Questions Allied Support on Iran After Italy Talks
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and questioned why allies including Italy were not backing U.S. efforts to confront Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Markets Understate Political Dysfunction After Reform Surge
item.description: British bond yields remained calm after Reform UK gains in local elections, but the rise of fringe parties bodes ill for economic policy coherence, with upstarts less likely than current government to deliver coherent policy. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Inflation Likely Rose to 3.8% as Fuel Costs Climb
item.description: India annual consumer inflation likely moved closer to the central bank 4% target in April as higher fuel costs from the U.S.-Iran conflict began feeding into prices, according to a Reuters poll of economists. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia and Ukraine agree to three-day ceasefire with prisoner swap
item.description: Russia and Ukraine confirmed a US-brokered three-day ceasefire from May 9-11, with a planned 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner swap. Trump expressed hope for an extension. (Reuters)

item.title: 🛡️ Ukraine running short of air defence missiles after Russian winter campaign
item.description: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles after Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as it braces for further strikes. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇹🇼 US concerned Taiwan defense delays are "concession" to China
item.description: The US State Department warned that further delays to Taiwan's military spending represent a 'concession' to China, as Taipei's defense ministry detailed the impact of projects excluded from a budget package. (Reuters)
